title: Performance Review Process and Self-Assessment Tips
text:
Q: What is the performance review process, timeline, and how can I effectively complete my self-assessment?
A: The performance review process occurs annually and is designed to evaluate employee performance, set goals, and identify development opportunities. Typically, reviews take place in the first quarter of the year, with self-assessments due one month prior to the review meetings. To complete your self-assessment effectively, reflect on your accomplishments, challenges, and areas for improvement over the past year. Use specific examples to illustrate your contributions and align them with company goals. Additionally, consider soliciting feedback from peers to gain a well-rounded perspective. Remember to set realistic and measurable goals for the upcoming year to demonstrate your commitment to growth.
